lesson.learn: Humans can hold their breath for extended periods due to several physiological adaptations.
lesson.simpler: Prolonged breath-holding is made possible by the body's ability to store oxygen in muscles and bloodstream.
lesson.keyTakeaway: The key to breath-hold performance lies in the efficient storage and utilization of oxygen in the body.
lesson.deeperDive: Research reveals that the body's adaptations enable it to conserve oxygen by reducing energy expenditure and increasing oxygen extraction from the bloodstream.
lesson.funFact: The longest recorded breath-hold in Guinness World Records is held by Ahmed Gabr, with a duration of 24 minutes and 3 seconds, set in 2014.

years.event: Enzo Maiorca becomes the first person to hold his breath for over 10 minutes
Ahmed Gabr sets new record for longest recorded breath-hold (24 minutes, 3 seconds)
Freediving begins gaining popularity worldwide with pioneers like Jacques Mayol and Enzo Maiorca leading the charge

concepts.name: Static Apnea Training (SAT)
concepts.description: A training method that aims to increase lung volume and reduce carbon dioxide levels, enabling longer breath-holds

concepts.name: Mitochondrial Function
concepts.description: The body's ability to store oxygen in muscles and bloodstream, and generate energy from stored oxygen
